channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Prime Minister Carney congratulates Jeremy Hansen on Artemis II mission
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney expressed his admiration for Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen during a live call with the Artemis II crew, highlighting Canada's pride in both Hansen's achievements and the ...

item.title: Dar City to resume training ahead of BAL finals in Kigali
item.description: Dar City basketball team is preparing to resume training on April 17 as they gear up for the Basketball Africa League (BAL) finals, set to be staged in Kigali, Rwanda from May 22 to 31. The team, whic...

item.title: Makoy entertains the audience with his energetic performance on It’s Showtime
item.description: On the April 9, 2026 episode of 'It’s Showtime,' Makoy, a lively contestant known for his charming personality and energetic dance style, captured the hearts of the Madlang People. His segment, descri...

item.title: Justice Othman explains reasons behind extension of October 29 Inquiry
item.description: The Chairperson of the Commission of Inquiry into the October 29, 2025 incidents, retired Chief Justice Othman, has provided a detailed explanation for the 21-day extension granted to complete the Com...

item.title: US Vice President Vance's Role Confirmed in Islamabad Talks After Iran's Direct Engagement
item.description: In a significant shift in US-Iran diplomacy, the White House confirmed that Vice President JD Vance will lead the American delegation in the upcoming talks in Islamabad. Initially, President Donald Tr...

item.title: Policy and Leadership Differences Between Taylor and Canavan Pose Challenges for the Coalition
item.description: The article examines the growing contrast between Liberal leader Angus Taylor and Nationals leader Matt Canavan, highlighting how their differing approaches could complicate the Coalition's strategy. ...

item.title: Four Dead in English Channel Crossing Attempt Off Northern France
item.description: Four people have tragically died after attempting to cross the English Channel in a small boat, according to French authorities. The incident occurred off the northern coast of France, near Boulogne, ...

item.title: Filipina Tech Advocate Kat Borlongan Receives France’s National Order of Merit
item.description: Kat Borlongan, a Filipina tech advocate, was recently honored as Knight of the National Order of Merit in France for her significant contributions to the country’s startup and technology ecosystems. A...

item.title: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini Clarify Resort Photos as Innocent
item.description: Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and NFL reporter Dianna Russini addressed recent viral photos showing them together at the Ambiente resort in Sedona, Arizona. The images, published by the New York Pos...

item.title: Dr Malasusa stresses forgiveness, love and peace as foundations for Tanzania's unity
item.description: The Head of the Evangelical Lutheran Church of Tanzania (ELCT), Dr Alex Malasusa, has encouraged Tanzanians to embrace forgiveness, love, and peace as central values for strengthening national unity a...

item.title: Impeachment Proceedings Against VP Sara Duterte Viewed as Accountability Test
item.description: According to Manila 3rd District Rep. Joel Chua, the impeachment proceedings against Vice President Sara Duterte aren't a distraction from current crises but a crucial preparation for the 2028 preside...

item.title: Indians head to polls in key state elections amid global tensions and energy concerns
item.description: Millions of Indians participated in voting across the states of Assam and Kerala on Thursday, marking the start of a significant series of state elections this month. These contests are being held at ...

item.title: Self-driving car kills duck in Texas neighborhood, raising concerns about autonomous vehicles
item.description: In Austin, Texas, a tragic incident involving a self-driving car sparked outrage among residents when an Avride autonomous vehicle killed a mother duck in the Mueller Lake neighborhood. The vehicle, w...

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire under strain as Israel launches major strikes on Lebanon
item.description: A fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, announced by President Donald Trump, is facing immediate challenges following large-scale Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon. The attacks, which Isra...

item.title: Ceasefire at Risk as Israel's Strikes on Lebanon Escalate Tensions
item.description: The ceasefire in the ongoing conflict in West Asia is under significant strain as Israel launched a major airstrike on Lebanon, killing over 250 people and injuring more than 1,000. Despite the ceasef...

item.title: NNPC Boosts Crude Oil Supply to Dangote Refinery Amidst Fuel Availability Concerns
item.description: The Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited (NNPC) don increase the amount of crude oil wey dem dey give Dangote Petroleum Refinery, reaching ten cargoes for March. This na serious boost for the h...

item.title: Young Tina's Struggle: Hiding Her Cigar-Smoking Cat from Her Father
item.description: In the story 'I Do So I Do' by Padmini Mongia, young Tina faces the dilemma of hiding her beloved cat, Fluffy, from her father Bhim. Fluffy, a sophisticated cat with a taste for cigars, is regularly p...

item.title: Senator Osotsi Attacked in Kisumu: Linda Mwananchi Denies Love Triangle Rumors
item.description: Things got really tense in Kisumu after Vihiiga Senator Godfrey Osotsi was brutally attacked by a large gang at a Java House. The incident, which happened at Western Mall, was initially surrounded by ...

item.title: Iran Open Temporary Safe Routes For Ships During Hormuz Truce
item.description: Iran don announce say dem go provide new safe routes for ships wey dey pass through the Strait of Hormuz, because say sea mines dey make the main area risky. The announcement come as part of a two-wee...

item.title: Pakistan assures tight security for foreign envoys ahead of key US‑Iran talks in Islamabad
